title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect the Patriots to acquire Eagles WR A.J. Brown after June 1 due to salary cap implications.
A.J. Brown, 28, has six 1,000-yard seasons and is a three-time Pro Bowler, making him a valuable target for New England.
The Eagles seek a first-round pick for Brown, with discussions hinging on potential New England concerns over Philadelphia's asking price.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' is streaming on Netflix, featuring a standout performance by Ralph Fiennes.
'Ken Burns Presents: Henry David Thoreau' documentary is streaming on PBS, with narration by George Clooney.
'Your Friends and Neighbors' Season 2 featuring Jon Hamm and James Marsden is now available on Apple TV.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox defeated the Padres 5-2 in their home opener, ending a five-game losing streak.
Caleb Durbin's RBI single broke his 0-for-19 season slump, kickstarting his performance.
Willson Contreras hit a home run, showcasing confidence and earning Fenway's approval.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots' phase one of offseason workouts starts on April 20, focusing on conditioning drills and meetings.
Patriots' Organized Team Activities (OTAs) will occur on May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11.
A mandatory minicamp for the Patriots will take place from June 15-17 following the OTA sessions.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots are projected to pick Texas A&M's Cashius Howell and Illinois' Gabe Jacas as edge rushers in ESPN's mock draft.
Howell recorded 11.5 sacks last season at Texas A&M, improving significantly from the previous year.
Jacas, seen as an NFL-ready run defender, increased his sack totals over four seasons, finishing with 11 last year.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Red Sox secured a 5-2 victory over the San Diego Padres in their home opener at Fenway Park.
Sonny Gray pitched six strong innings, and Willson Contreras hit the winning home run in the sixth inning.
Caleb Durbin broke his hitless streak with an RBI single, contributing to the team's much-needed fresh start.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover officials are seeking grant funding for a new police body-worn camera program after the Fitzsimmons shooting.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons was acquitted after an incident without body camera footage, resulting in calls for camera implementation.
Massachusetts has provided $13 million in grants to local police for body-worn camera programs since 2021.

title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts returned to Fenway Park, reflecting on the changes since leaving the Red Sox for the Padres.
Bogaerts received a standing ovation from Red Sox fans during his first at-bat at Fenway as a Padres player.
Bogaerts highlighted the pressure on 21-year-old Roman Anthony as a potential new face for the Red Sox.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Freedom Trail in Boston, a 2.5-mile path with 16 historic sites, attracts over 4 million visitors yearly.
Six Flags New England introduces the Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, opening April 11 for season pass holders.
Restaurant Week Portsmouth & the Seacoast offers prix fixe menus from April 9-18, with prices ranging from $32 to $52.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton's noise complaint against Warwick neighbors reached the Rhode Island Supreme Court for an appeal.
Carlton claims noise from adjacent industrial property, leased by North American Crane & Rigging LLC, is relentless and disruptive.
Defendants argue the noise injunction is vague and continue challenging a 2025 zoning decision affecting property use.
